Tren de Aragua.description: Transnational criminal organization originating from Aragua state, Venezuela, based in Tocorón prison. Founded around 2009-2010, it engages in human trafficking, drug trafficking, extortion, and murder. Led by Héctor 'Niño' Guerrero, it is considered the most powerful criminal group in Venezuela, with operations expanding to several Latin American countries and the United States. Designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. in 2025.

Los Huistas.description: Los Huistas are a powerful Guatemalan criminal organization based in Huehuetenango, near the Mexican border. Founded in the late 1990s, they control a strategic drug trafficking route for cocaine, methamphetamine, and heroin from Central America to the United States. They have evolved from transporters to producers of methamphetamine and money launderers, infiltrating state institutions. The group operates under a centralized hierarchy and was led by Eugenio Darío Molina-López until his capture in 2026.

edges.evidence_quote: En 2024-2025, surgieron tensiones cuando Los Huistas comenzaron a cobrar "impuestos" por el paso de drogas a otros grupos locales, como la banda de "Tío Balde". Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala, buscando hacer frente a la hegemonía de Los Huistas.

descriptions: Los Huistas act as logistical partners for CJNG, transporting drugs.

'Ndrina Pino.description: A 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Cosenza, Calabria, Italy. It was formed after the death of Luigi Palermo in 1977 and split into two factions, leading to a long feud with the Perna clan. The clan was involved in drug trafficking, arms possession, and international criminal activities, including a bid for the construction of Bucharest airport in Romania. In 1995, boss Franco Pino became a pentito (collaborator), leading to arrests. As of the 2000s, the clan has merged with the Perna group.

As of 2022, it remains active with an estimated 400-500 members.

Ang Soon Tong.description: Ang Soon Tong is a secret society and gang based in Singapore and Malaysia, active since the 1950s. It operates mainly in the Sembawang area of Singapore and engages in drug trafficking, arms smuggling, illegal moneylending, and illegal gambling. The gang has been involved in violent clashes and murders, with members receiving death sentences and imprisonment. It remains active as of the 2020s.

Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips.description: The Rollin' 30s Harlem Crips are a set of the Crips alliance, formed by Belizean American Crips who moved from South Los Angeles to Belize and then to Harlem, New York. Established in New York City in the late 1980s or early 1990s by Dalmin "Diamond" Mayen and associates, the gang is involved in drug trafficking, weapon trafficking, prostitution, murder, and robbery. It operates in Los Angeles, New York City, and Belize City, with an estimated membership of 700-1,000.

The Brotherhood of Eternal Love.description: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love was an organization of drug users and distributors operating from the mid-1960s through the late 1970s in Orange County, California. Founded by John Griggs, it produced and distributed LSD and hashish with the goal of starting a 'psychedelic revolution' in the United States. The group was known for manufacturing 'Orange Sunshine' LSD and assisting Timothy Leary's escape from prison. It was largely dismantled after a major drug raid in 1972, though some members were arrested as late as 2009.

descriptions: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love was formed by members of the Street Sweepers gang, who reincorporated as the new organization.
edges.evidence_quote: In the mid-1960s, John Griggs and others were members of a car gang called the Street Sweepers in Anaheim, California. ... he and the other Street Sweepers became psychedelic evangelists and reincorporated themselves as the Brotherhood of Eternal Love.

descriptions: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love hired the Weather Underground to assist Timothy Leary's escape.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1970 The Brotherhood of Eternal Love hired the radical left organization Weather Underground for a fee of $25,000 to help Timothy Leary make his way to Algeria after he escaped from prison.

descriptions: Weather Underground was paid by The Brotherhood of Eternal Love to break Timothy Leary out of prison.
edges.evidence_quote: the group accepted a $25,000 payment from the largest international psychedelic drug distribution organization, called The Brotherhood of Eternal Love, to break LSD advocate Timothy Leary out of a California prison

Lazarus Group.description: State-sponsored hacker group alleged to be run by the North Korean government. Known for cyberattacks since the 2000s, including the 2014 Sony Pictures hack and the 2016 Bangladesh Bank heist. Designated as an advanced persistent threat, it engages in cyberespionage, cyberwarfare, and financial theft. Based in Pyongyang, North Korea.
